items.title: What the Numbers Say About FIFA 2026 Cyber Risk
items.summary: The FIFA World Cup 2026 opened on June 11. By that date, according to Check Point Research, the fraud infrastructure targeting it had already been built, staged, and partially deployed....

items.title: Attackers Exploit SimpleHelp CVE-2026-48558 to Deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer
items.summary: An unknown threat actor has been observed exploiting a recently disclosed maximum-severity security flaw in SimpleHelp to deliver two previously unreported malware families, TaskWeaver...

items.title: AirDrop and Quick Share Flaws Let Nearby Attackers Trigger Crashes and Bypass Checks
items.summary: Two researchers have found six security flaws in AirDrop and Quick Share, the wireless features that beam files between nearby devices with no cables or shared network. An attacker...

items.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
items.summary: Convince an AI browser that it is playing a game, and it can hand over your login details. That is the finding behind BioShocking, a technique from security firm LayerX that tricked six...

items.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API. The...

items.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
